name: Midwest Regional Bank
description_short: Missouri-based full-service bank operating since 1894 with $1B+ in assets, offering checking, savings, business loans, and SBA lending across multiple states.
description_long:
Midwest Regional Bank is a Missouri state-chartered bank with a 130-year operational history, founded in 1894. The institution has grown to assets exceeding $1 billion and maintains physical locations across Missouri (Festus, Arnold, Chesterfield, Sunset Hills, Clayton, Kirkwood, and Otterville) with lending offices in Arizona, Colorado, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Texas. The bank positions itself as a comprehensive financial services provider serving retail, commercial, and agricultural markets.

The bank offers a broad range of retail and commercial banking products. For consumers, these include checking accounts, smart savings accounts, CDs, IRA accounts, and credit cards with competitive rates and flexible credit limits. For businesses, Midwest Regional Bank emphasizes SBA lending expertise, small business solutions, treasury services (receivables collection, fraud reduction, account monitoring, and investment maximization), and commercial lending across multiple states. The bank also provides online banking platforms for both personal and business accounts, a mobile app, and free MoneyPass ATM access.

The bank distinguishes itself through a stated "Satisfaction Guarantee" policy that promises direct escalation to President Tyler Bender for unresolved customer complaints, positioning itself as offering personalized service without automated responses. The emphasis on SBA lending expertise and the multi-state lending presence differentiate it from smaller regional banks. The institution markets itself as serving agricultural markets alongside retail and commercial sectors, suggesting broader rural and agribusiness focus.

The company is FDIC-insured and an Equal Housing Lender, providing baseline federal protections. However, the website provides limited detail on specific interest rates, fees, minimum balance requirements, or account terms. The company appears to require phone contact ((636) 937-5351) or email for most detailed product information, which may be less convenient than fully transparent online disclosure. No independent third-party ratings or customer satisfaction data are visible on the website itself.
services: Personal savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Credit cards with variable credit limits
Small business checking and savings solutions
Commercial treasury services (receivables collection, fraud monitoring, account monitoring)
Personal and business online banking platforms
Business lending across multiple states (AZ, CO, FL, NC, SC, TN, TX)
pros: FDIC-insured member bank with $1B+ in assets and 130-year operational history providing institutional stability
Stated 'Satisfaction Guarantee' with direct escalation to bank president for unresolved complaints
Multi-state SBA lending expertise with dedicated lending offices in 7 states beyond Missouri
Free MoneyPass ATM network access for cardholders
Comprehensive treasury services for business customers including fraud monitoring and receivables collection
Serves agricultural, retail, and commercial markets with specialized solutions for each sector
Mobile app and online banking platforms for personal and business accounts
cons: Website lacks specific interest rates, APRs, annual fees, or minimum balance requirements for any products
Limited online account opening or product application options; requires phone calls or email for detailed information
No visible customer reviews, ratings, or third-party satisfaction metrics on the website
Physical branch presence limited to Missouri and a few other states, not a nationwide bank
Product terms and conditions not fully disclosed on website; must contact bank directly for specifics
best_for: Small to mid-sized business owners seeking SBA lending and treasury services across multiple states
Missouri residents and businesses in Festus, Arnold, Chesterfield, Clayton, Kirkwood, and surrounding areas seeking local banking relationships
Agricultural businesses and farmers needing specialized commercial banking solutions
Customers willing to call or email for detailed product information rather than preferring full online transparency
diagnosis: Midwest Regional Bank is best suited for small-to-mid-sized business owners, particularly those needing SBA financing or treasury services, and Missouri-based consumers and agricultural businesses seeking a full-service regional bank with local relationships. The main caveat is that the bank requires direct contact for specific pricing and terms rather than providing transparent online disclosures, and it lacks the nationwide convenience of larger national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
